reasoning_model_1: The audio content accurately follows the script 'This proposal, good, we have approved it.' without any errors or omissions. Regarding the required paralinguistic feature (Pause), the model successfully executes the instruction. Following the word 'good' (approx. 1.5s), there is a distinct pause before the phrase 'we have approved it' begins. The prosody is natural, and the pause serves the syntactic structure of the sentence as requested, clearly separating the interjection 'good' from the main clause. The tone is appropriate and the pause is of sufficient duration to be noticeable without breaking the flow unnaturaly.
reasoning_model_2: The model generates the audio content 'This proposal, good, we have approved it.' with perfect accuracy relative to the script. In terms of the feature control, the model adheres to the command by placing a clear pause after the word 'good'. The pacing is natural, and the silence after 'good' creates the intended emphasis and separation before 'we have approved it'. The transition out of the pause is smooth, maintaining high fluency and naturalness throughout the utterance.
model_comparison: Both models demonstrated excellent performance in this evaluation. They both accurately synthesized the text 'This proposal, good, we have approved it.' and successfully implemented the specific paralinguistic instruction to include a clear pause after the word 'good'. In both cases, the pause was distinct and audibly clear, effectively separating the segments of the sentence as requested. The fluency and naturalness of both generations were high, with no robotic artifacts or mispronunciations. Since both models fully satisfied the core requirement (the pause) and the content accuracy requirement without any discernible flaws or significant differences in quality, the comparison results in an even tie.
